title: helianthemum apenninum subsp. stoechadifolium
intro: Helianthemum apenninum subsp. stoechadifolium, also known as Rockrose, is a type of flowering plant in the Cistaceae family. It is used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, thanks to its astringent, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The Rockrose plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, offering potential health benefits for those who consume it.
